description: You are a tracking engineer preparing for an International Laser Ranging Service (ILRS) campaign. Configure GPredict to track geodetic spherical satellites and enforce laser safety constraints. Add the 'Matera_SLR' station (40.6496 N, 16.7046 E, 536m altitude) and the 'Yarragadee_SLR' station (29.0465 S, 115.3470 E, 248m altitude). Create a tracking module named 'ILRS_Targets' containing LAGEOS-1 (NORAD 08820), LAGEOS-2 (NORAD 22195), STARLETTE (NORAD 07646), STELLA (NORAD 22823), and LARES (NORAD 38077). Set the minimum elevation for pass predictions to 30 degrees and ensure the application is set to display Metric units. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

natural_language.prompt: I need to set up GPredict for our upcoming International Laser Ranging Service (ILRS) campaign. First, add our two laser observatories as ground stations: Matera in Italy (40.6496 N, 16.7046 E, 536m) and Yarragadee in Australia (29.0465 South, 115.3470 East, 248m). Be careful with Yarragadee, it's in the Southern Hemisphere. Second, create a new tracking module named 'ILRS_Targets' with our 5 cannonball satellites: LAGEOS-1 (8820), LAGEOS-2 (22195), STARLETTE (7646), STELLA (22823), and LARES (38077). Third, for laser safety, we can't shoot low on the horizon. Please go into the Predict preferences and set the minimum elevation angle to 30 degrees. Finally, make sure the software is using metric units.
